settings.title: Workspace Settings
settings.subtitle: Tune the shell, editor, and runtime defaults
settings.description: The interface supports both dark and light shells, while Monaco keeps its own theme pipeline for code editing. Changes are saved automatically.
settings.footer: Settings persist locally across desktop and web sessions.
about.title: About
about.description: Product identity, version metadata, and release entry points for this build.
about.hero.copy: Lingua keeps fast multi-language experiments inside one focused editor shell.
about.field.versionHint: Desktop reads the packaged app version; web falls back to bundled metadata.
about.field.buildDateHint: Approximate bundle timestamp for the current build.
about.links.label: Project links
about.links.hint: Open the canonical repository, website, or license document.
about.actions.label: Release actions
about.actions.hint: Jump into updates, release notes, or onboarding from the identity section.
whatsNew.title: What's New
whatsNew.subtitle: Release notes and recent changes
whatsNew.description: Review the latest shipped changes first, then expand older release notes when you need more context.
whatsNew.empty: No release notes are bundled with this build yet.
tour.step.editor.title: Editor workspace
tour.step.editor.text: This is the main coding surface. Templates, inline results, and runtime markers all stay anchored to the editor so you can iterate without leaving the workspace.
tour.step.run.title: Run the active file
tour.step.run.text: Click Run to continue. The tour waits for a real execution here so you can see the feedback loop instead of skipping past it.
tour.step.console.title: Console feedback
tour.step.console.text: Logs, warnings, errors, and timing badges land here. When execution emits structured output, Lingua keeps the console and inline panel in sync.
tour.step.explorer.title: Project explorer
tour.step.explorer.text: Use the explorer to open folders, create files, and keep tabs tied to the working tree instead of isolated scratch files.
tour.step.toolbar.title: Toolbar controls
tour.step.toolbar.text: Quick-open, command palette, snippets, console visibility, and settings all live in the top toolbar so the core workflow stays one click away.
tour.step.snippets.title: Snippet library
tour.step.snippets.text: Save useful code from the active tab into reusable snippets, then reopen it later in a new tab or insert it into the current file.
tour.step.commandPalette.title: Command palette
tour.step.commandPalette.text: The command palette is the fastest way to navigate templates, snippets, layout changes, and actions. Open it anytime with Cmd+Shift+P or Ctrl+Shift+P.
appearance.title: Appearance
appearance.description: Lingua supports a dark-first shell and a refined light mode without changing your editor runtime settings.
appearance.theme.dark.label: Dark
appearance.theme.dark.description: High-contrast graphite shell for focused coding sessions.
appearance.theme.light.label: Light
appearance.theme.light.description: Warm paper workspace with calmer contrast and stronger daylight readability.
layout.title: Layout
layout.description: Choose how the editor and console share space inside the main workstation.
layout.preset.horizontal.label: Horizontal Split
layout.preset.vertical.label: Vertical Split
layout.preset.editorOnly.label: Editor Only
editor.title: Editor
editor.description: Keep the Monaco surface aligned with how you read code and how aggressively you want automatic safeguards.
editor.theme.label: Editor theme
editor.theme.hint: Controls Monaco only. App shell theme stays independent.
editor.fontFamily.label: Font family
editor.fontFamily.hint: Use a coding typeface that matches your workflow.
editor.fontSize.label: Font size
editor.fontSize.hint: Adjust the editor scale without touching the shell density.
editor.lineNumbers.label: Line numbers
editor.lineNumbers.hint: Keep gutter references visible while editing.
editor.wordWrap.label: Word wrap
editor.wordWrap.hint: Wrap long lines inside the viewport.
editor.minimap.label: Minimap
editor.minimap.hint: Show the code overview strip on the right edge.
editor.loopProtection.label: Loop protection
editor.loopProtection.hint: Stops runaway JS and TS loops before they lock the renderer.
editor.maxIterations.label: Max iterations
editor.maxIterations.hint: Caps the injected loop guard for inline JS, TS, and Python runners.
editor.restoreSession.label: Reopen last session
editor.restoreSession.hint: Restore tabs from the previous session on restart.
updates.title: Updates
updates.description: Desktop update status is surfaced here; web builds show capability limits explicitly.
updates.status.label: Status
updates.status.hint: Review the current desktop update state and any runtime messages.
updates.actions.label: Actions
updates.actions.hint: These controls stay disabled when updates are not supported.
updates.message.webUnavailable: Automatic updates are not available in the web version.
plugins.title: Plugins
plugins.description: Local plugin discovery is surfaced as an operational panel rather than a marketplace promise.
plugins.directory.label: Local plugin directory
language.label: Language
language.hint: Choose the display language for the Lingua interface.
toolbar.run.title: Run (Cmd+Enter)
toolbar.run.label: Run
console.title: Console
console.empty.filtered: No entries match the active filters.
results.inline.title: Inline Result
results.output.title: Program Output
results.empty.inline: Results appear here as you type.
results.empty.output: Output appears after the current program finishes.
emptyState.headline: Run experiments fast, keep the workspace calm, and stay inside one editor.
emptyState.description: Start a blank file, open a language template, or jump straight to a snippet. The shell is tuned for tight feedback loops and native desktop execution when you need it.
emptyState.startingPoints.title: Starting Points
emptyState.startingPoints.description: Open a proven template and iterate.
